region_of_origin: United States / Singapore fast food adaptation

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has an entry for fried chicken food product (FOODON_00002372); this record is a composite set meal including fried chicken, french fries, and coleslaw. Closest parent match used for the primary protein component.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken, fried — composite meal approximation under poultry meat preparations
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated composite code for fried chicken set meal with fries and coleslaw. A00GJ (fried poultry) used as the closest defensible anchor for the primary component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite fast-food set meal.

serving.description: 1 set meal (2 pieces fried chicken + 1 medium packet fries + coleslaw)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal consisting of 2 pieces of fried chicken (approximately 200g), one medium packet of french fries (approximately 150g), and a serving of coleslaw (approximately 100g), totalling approximately 450g.

nutrient_source.title: Fried chicken with french fries and coleslaw — HPB Singapore Foodo Database (F08020023)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record F08020023 for this composite set meal. All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB source with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (fried chicken + fries + coleslaw) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite set meal. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on weighted analogy: deep-fried battered chicken (GI ~45, low contribution due to protein-fat dominance) and french fries (GI ~75, high-GI dominant component per University of Sydney GI Database). Coleslaw contributes negligible carbohydrate load. Composite weighted GI approximated at 65 reflecting fries as the dominant carbohydrate source. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × carbohydrate per serving (68.3g from 450g serving × 15.17g/100g) / 100 = 44.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 441mg/100g; estimated 1985mg per full set meal serving
hypertension.guidance: This set meal delivers approximately 1985mg sodium per serving — approaching the WHO daily limit of 2000mg in a single meal — and individuals with hypertension should avoid this meal or request reduced-salt preparation on both the chicken marinade and fries, and limit or omit the mayonnaise-based coleslaw dressing.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI french fries dominate carbohydrate load (GI ~75); total GL per serving ~44
type2_diabetes.guidance: The high glycaemic load of approximately 44 per serving, driven primarily by deep-fried potato fries (GI ~75), poses a significant postprandial glucose spike risk for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, and this meal should be avoided or the fries substituted with a non-starchy vegetable side to substantially reduce glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat ~3.2g/100g, trans fat ~0.3g/100g, sodium 441mg/100g, deep-fried preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat (~14.4g per serving), trans fat (~1.35g per serving from deep-frying), and high sodium (~1985mg per serving) makes this meal a high cardiovascular risk food; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption and choose grilled chicken with salad as an alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Trans fat ~0.3g/100g, dual deep-frying AGE load, nightshade potato
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Deep-frying of both chicken and potato generates a high advanced glycation end-product (AGE) load from two sources, trans fat at ~0.3g/100g is strongly pro-inflammatory, and potato as a nightshade vegetable may exacerbate solanine-related joint inflammation in sensitive individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried Chicken (2 pieces) with French Fries and Coleslaw is a Western-style fast-food set meal widely available in Singapore, consisting of two pieces of deep-fried battered chicken, one medium packet of deep-fried potato french fries, and a portion of coleslaw made from shredded cabbage and carrot dressed with mayonnaise. The chicken is coated in a seasoned wheat flour batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy, yielding a juicy interior with a crunchy exterior. The french fries are crisp, salted, and high in starch. The coleslaw provides a creamy, slightly tangy contrast. Per 100g, this composite meal provides 223 kcal, 10.89g protein, 13.19g fat, 15.17g carbohydrate, 1.88g sugar, 1.47g dietary fibre, and 441mg sodium. The meal is calorie-dense and high in sodium, saturated fat, and trans fat from deep-frying. It carries a high glycaemic load (~44 per serving) driven by the french fries. Clinically, this meal is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension (high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high GI and GL from fries), cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, sodium), or rheumatoid arthritis (AGE load, trans fat, nightshade potato). It is halal-certified in most Singapore fast-food contexts. Common serving size is approximately 450g for the full set.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record F08020023 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy and documented. Trans fat estimated from published deep-frying literature and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database values for french fries and fried chicken components, clearly documented.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown for this composite set meal (fried chicken + fries + coleslaw) is not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database record F08020023, and no sufficiently close composite analogue exists in USDA FoodData Central to provide defensible values for all these fields as of 2026. Saturated fat (3.2g/100g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried battered chicken and french fries in approximate meal proportions (2:1.5:1 chicken:fries:coleslaw by weight). Trans fat (0.3g/100g) estimated from published literature on deep-frying trans fat generation in commercial fast-food settings; this value is consistent with the input health_suitability assessment. No core macro is zero; all macros are non-zero and fully supported. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate weighted from University of Sydney GI Database values: french fries GI ~75 (dominant carbohydrate source) and fried chicken GI ~45 (protein-fat dominant, low carbohydrate contribution). Glycemic load per serving = 65 × (450 × 0.1517) / 100 = 65 × 68.3 / 100 = 44.4. This record is classified as a Singaporean-context Western set meal; multilingual names provided in English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per G6 mandatory language rules for Singapore-served dishes. Aliases consistency self-check: all strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array — confirmed consistent.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name present. Multilingual names provided in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi satisfying G6 for Singapore-served Western dishes.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (223.0), protein_g (10.89), fat_g (13.19), carbohydrate_g (15.17) all present, numeric, and sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo F08020023 with confidence 0.95.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.47), sodium_mg (441.0), saturated_fat_g (3.2 — estimated from USDA analogy, documented in notes), sugar_g (1.88) all present and numeric.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (65), gi_category (medium), glycemic_load_per_serving (44.4), gi_source all present. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database values for component foods, fully documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002372 (fried chicken food product) used as closest_parent anchor with gap note. FoodEx2 A00GJ used as closest_parent anchor with gap note. SNOMED CT null with gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters, covers food identity, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes (human_condition_name correct), and Cardiovascular Disease all present with concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentences. Rheumatoid arthritis included as additional condition.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: All 12 strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. Hindi entry corrected to remove erroneous mid-word line break present in previous draft. Consistency check passed.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Updated ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'; documented all -1 fields in notes with data-unavailability justification citing HPB F08020023 and USDA FoodData Central.

Iteration 3: Fixed q8 aliases consistency — verified every string in aliases_by_language appears verbatim in flat aliases array; corrected Hindi alias string to remove erroneous mid-word character from previous draft; confirmed all 12 aliases_by_language entries present in flat aliases list.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record F08020023 (Fried Chicken 2 pieces with French Fries and Coleslaw) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8. Core macros sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record F08020023. Saturated fat and trans fat estimated from USDA and literature with full documentation. All -1 fields documented in notes with source-confirmed unavailability basis. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database component analogues. Multilingual coverage satisfies G6 for Singapore-served Western dishes (English, Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Aliases consistency verified: all aliases_by_language entries confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases array. Ontology uses honest closest_parent matches with gap notes. Health context covers all three mandatory conditions with specific clinical guidance.
